untranslatable_reason: This signal is a capital allocation and portfolio management rule, not a price-action trading signal. It describes: (1) dividing account equity into a leveraged trading subaccount (D fraction) and cash reserve (1-D fraction), (2) applying leverage multiplier f to the subaccount, (3) reallocating capital when new equity highs are reached, and (4) preserving capital during drawdowns. These are meta-level position-sizing and account-management directives that operate on account equity, drawdown tracking, and high-water mark logic—not on price indicators or technical conditions. A backtest engine with price-based indicator rules cannot execute this signal without custom accounting logic for equity tracking, leverage management, and dynamic capital reallocation between subaccounts.
translation_notes: Signal ID CHAN_AT_DRY_9 requires implementation at the portfolio/account management layer, not the trade entry/exit layer. Parameters D (equity allocation fraction), f (leverage multiplier), and high-water mark tracking must be handled by backtest engine infrastructure for position sizing and account reconciliation, not by condition-based rules on price data.
